Genes.Effect_Direction: Expression of GuApiGT → almost complete conversion of liquiritin (1) and isoliquiritin (2) to their 2''-O-apiosides (1a and 2a); strict selectivity for UDP-apiose over 7 other sugar donors; Km = 2.59 ± 0.23 µmol/L for isoliquiritin; kcat = 0.11 s⁻¹; 37 out of 65 substrates accepted; de novo biosynthesis in tobacco yielded 5.46 mg/g DW (1a) and 4.73 mg/g DW (2a)
Genes.Evidence_Text: MSTRG.23171.4 almost completely converted liquiritin (1) and isoliquiritin (2) into their 2''-O-apiosides 1a and 2a, respectively (Fig. 2b). Although UAXS could also produce UDP-Xyl, no products were observed when UDP-Xyl was added. GuApiGT showed strict sugar donor selectivity towards UDP-Api. It could not recognize seven other donors (Fig. 3c). The RLGSDH motif is critical for selectivity. De novo biosynthesis in tobacco yielded 5.46 and 4.73 mg/g DW for 1a and 2a.
Genes.Evidence_Type: Genetic perturbation + metabolite quantification (LC/MS, NMR); enzyme kinetics; crystal structure; mutagenesis; de novo biosynthesis in tobacco
Genes.Final_Nutrient_Product: Flavonoid apiosides (liquiritin apioside, isoliquiritin apioside, and other flavonoid 2''-O-apiosides)
Genes.Intermediate_Metabolites_Affected: Converts flavonoid glycosides (liquiritin, isoliquiritin, and 35 other glycoside substrates) to their 2''-O-apiosides
Genes.Pathway: Flavonoid apioside biosynthesis; catalyzes 2''-O-apiosylation of flavonoid glycosides using UDP-apiose as sugar donor
Genes.Perturbation_Type: Heterologous expression in E. coli (in vitro enzyme assay); transient expression in Nicotiana benthamiana (de novo biosynthesis); site-directed mutagenesis (RLGSDH motif); crystal structure (PDB: 8HZZ)
Genes.Quantitative_Effect: Near 100% conversion for liquiritin and isoliquiritin; >80% conversion for 14 substrates; de novo yields: 5.46 mg/g DW (liquiritin apioside), 4.73 mg/g DW (isoliquiritin apioside); up to ~6 mg/g DW for other apiosides
Genes.Tissue_Stage: Roots (highest expression); also expressed in cortex and leaves

Genes.Effect_Direction: UAXS expression → provides UDP-Api → enables apiosylation; absence of module 2 → no flavonoid apioside production
Genes.Evidence_Text: UAXS was co-infiltrated into tobacco with GuApiGT to supplement the UDP-apiose donor. When module 1 or 2 was absent, no 2a could be generated. It was generated after UDP-GlcA or 2' was injected into the tobacco leaves (Fig. 8c).
Genes.Evidence_Type: Genetic perturbation + metabolite quantification (module removal experiment in tobacco; in vitro enzymatic assay)
Genes.Final_Nutrient_Product: Flavonoid apiosides (provides essential UDP-apiose sugar donor)
Genes.Gene_Role_Category: Key enzyme gene (sugar nucleotide biosynthesis); branch/flux controller
Genes.Pathway: UDP-apiose biosynthesis; converts UDP-glucuronic acid (UDP-GlcA) to UDP-apiose (UDP-Api) and UDP-xylose (UDP-Xyl)
Genes.Perturbation_Type: Co-expression with GuApiGT in vitro (UDP-Api supply system) and in tobacco (module 2 of de novo biosynthesis); when module 2 absent, no apiosides produced
Genes.Quantitative_Effect: Essential for apioside production; when module 2 absent, no 2a generated; when UDP-GlcA injected into tobacco with modules, 2a was produced
Genes.Species: Arabidopsis thaliana (used in G. uralensis and N. benthamiana systems)
Genes.Tissue_Stage: Used in vitro and in tobacco transient expression (module 2)

Genes.Effect_Direction: GuGT53 expression → regio-selective 4'/4-O-glucosylation → improved yield of isoliquiritin apioside compared to GuGT14 (which produced byproduct 7a due to poor regio-selectivity)
Genes.Evidence_Text: GuGT53 could regio-selectively and efficiently catalyze 4'/4-O-glycosylation of liquiritigenin (1') and 2' into liquiritin (1) and isoliquiritin (2), respectively (Supplementary Fig. 146). The yield of isoliquiritin apioside was improved when GuGT14 was replaced by GuGT53.
Genes.Evidence_Type: Genetic perturbation + metabolite quantification (LC/MS); comparison with GuGT14 in tobacco
Genes.Final_Nutrient_Product: Flavonoid apiosides (produces the glycoside intermediates liquiritin and isoliquiritin, which are direct substrates for GuApiGT)
Genes.Pathway: Flavonoid glycoside biosynthesis; catalyzes regio-selective 4'/4-O-glucosylation of liquiritigenin and isoliquiritigenin
Genes.Perturbation_Type: Heterologous expression in E. coli (enzyme assay); replacement of GuGT14 in tobacco de novo biosynthesis (module 3)
Genes.Quantitative_Effect: Replacing GuGT14 with GuGT53 improved isoliquiritin apioside yield (reduced byproduct 7a formation)
Genes.Tissue_Stage: Roots (similar expression pattern as GuCHS, GuCHR, and GuApiGT)

Genes.Effect_Direction: GuGT14 expression → production of isoliquiritin apioside (2a) but also major byproduct 7a due to poor regio-selectivity and endogenous tobacco GTs
Genes.Evidence_Text: 7a was generated as a major byproduct when GuGT14 was used. It was due to the poor regio-selectivity of GuGT14 and endogenous glycosyltransferases from tobacco (Fig. 8c and Supplementary Fig. 145).
Genes.Evidence_Type: Genetic perturbation + metabolite quantification (LC/MS in tobacco)
Genes.Final_Nutrient_Product: Flavonoid apiosides (produces glycoside intermediates but with poor regio-selectivity)
Genes.Pathway: Flavonoid glycoside biosynthesis; catalyzes O-glucosylation of flavonoid aglycones
Genes.Perturbation_Type: Transient expression in N. benthamiana (module 3); compared with GuGT53
Genes.Quantitative_Effect: Generated 7a as major byproduct; replaced by GuGT53 for improved yield
Genes.Tissue_Stage: Used in tobacco de novo biosynthesis (module 3)

Genes.Effect_Direction: GuCHR expression in module 1 → production of isoliquiritigenin → enables downstream glycosylation and apiosylation
Genes.Evidence_Text: AtPAL, AtC4H, At4CL, AtCHS and GuCHR were used to synthesize isoliquiritigenin (2'). When module 1 or 2 was absent, no 2a could be generated (Fig. 8c).
Genes.Evidence_Type: Genetic perturbation + metabolite quantification (module removal experiment in tobacco)
Genes.Final_Nutrient_Product: Flavonoid apiosides (isoliquiritin apioside and liquiritin apioside)
Genes.Pathway: Flavonoid biosynthesis; converts naringenin chalcone to isoliquiritigenin (the precursor aglycone for isoliquiritin apioside)
Genes.Perturbation_Type: Transient expression in N. benthamiana (module 1); when module 1 absent, no apiosides produced
Genes.Quantitative_Effect: Essential component of module 1; absence of module 1 → no 2a production
Genes.Tissue_Stage: Roots (high expression); used in tobacco de novo biosynthesis (module 1)

Genes.Effect_Direction: GuCHI expression → enabled production of liquiritin apioside (1a); AtCHI could NOT produce 1a
Genes.Evidence_Text: We cloned GuCHI from G. uralensis to replace AtCHI, as 1a could not be detected when AtCHI was used in module 1 (Supplementary Fig. 147).
Genes.Evidence_Type: Genetic perturbation + metabolite quantification (gene replacement experiment in tobacco)
Genes.Pathway: Flavonoid biosynthesis; isomerizes isoliquiritigenin (chalcone) to liquiritigenin (flavanone)
Genes.Perturbation_Type: Transient expression in N. benthamiana; compared with AtCHI
Genes.Quantitative_Effect: 1a could not be detected when AtCHI was used; GuCHI replacement enabled 1a production (5.46 mg/g DW)
Genes.Tissue_Stage: Used in tobacco de novo biosynthesis (module 1)

Genes.Effect_Direction: PtApiGT expression → apiosylation of flavonoid glycosides confirmed
Genes.Evidence_Text: We cloned PtApiGT, SsApiGT, GgApiGT, and GiApiGT from the plants, and identified them as apiosyltransferases by enzyme catalysis reactions (Fig. 7c and Supplementary Fig. 142).
Genes.Pathway: Flavonoid apioside biosynthesis; catalyzes 2''-O-apiosylation of flavonoid glycosides
Genes.Perturbation_Type: Heterologous expression in E. coli; enzyme catalysis reaction
Genes.Quantitative_Effect: NA (functional identification confirmed by enzyme catalysis, Fig. 7c)

Genes.Effect_Direction: SsApiGT expression → apiosylation of flavonoid glycosides confirmed
Genes.Evidence_Text: We cloned PtApiGT, SsApiGT, GgApiGT, and GiApiGT from the plants, and identified them as apiosyltransferases by enzyme catalysis reactions (Fig. 7c and Supplementary Fig. 142).
Genes.Pathway: Flavonoid apioside biosynthesis; catalyzes 2''-O-apiosylation of flavonoid glycosides
Genes.Perturbation_Type: Heterologous expression in E. coli (gene synthesized); enzyme catalysis reaction
Genes.Quantitative_Effect: NA (functional identification confirmed by enzyme catalysis, Fig. 7c)

Genes.Effect_Direction: GgApiGT expression → apiosylation of flavonoid glycosides confirmed
Genes.Evidence_Text: We cloned PtApiGT, SsApiGT, GgApiGT, and GiApiGT from the plants, and identified them as apiosyltransferases by enzyme catalysis reactions (Fig. 7c and Supplementary Fig. 142).
Genes.Pathway: Flavonoid apioside biosynthesis; catalyzes 2''-O-apiosylation of flavonoid glycosides
Genes.Perturbation_Type: Heterologous expression in E. coli; enzyme catalysis reaction
Genes.Quantitative_Effect: NA (functional identification confirmed by enzyme catalysis, Fig. 7c)

Genes.Effect_Direction: GiApiGT expression → apiosylation of flavonoid glycosides confirmed
Genes.Evidence_Text: We cloned PtApiGT, SsApiGT, GgApiGT, and GiApiGT from the plants, and identified them as apiosyltransferases by enzyme catalysis reactions (Fig. 7c and Supplementary Fig. 142).
Genes.Pathway: Flavonoid apioside biosynthesis; catalyzes 2''-O-apiosylation of flavonoid glycosides
Genes.Perturbation_Type: Heterologous expression in E. coli; enzyme catalysis reaction
Genes.Quantitative_Effect: NA (functional identification confirmed by enzyme catalysis, Fig. 7c)

Genes.Effect_Direction: WT Sb3GT1 cannot use UDP-Api → mutants (375S/Q377H, F372R/Q376H, F372R/375S/Q377H) gained apiosylation activity → produced kaempferol 3-O-apioside
Genes.Evidence_Text: We inserted a serine residue into the motif and constructed the 375S/Q377H mutant of Sb3GT1, as well as the F372R/Q376H and F372R/375S/Q377H mutants. All the three mutants could catalyze kaempferol (66) into its 3-O-apioside (Fig. 6b and Supplementary Fig. 140).
Genes.Evidence_Type: Genetic perturbation (mutagenesis) + metabolite quantification (LC/MS); crystal structure
Genes.Gene_Role_Category: Key enzyme gene (flavonoid 3-O-glycosyltransferase); protein engineering target
Genes.Pathway: Flavonoid glycoside biosynthesis; WT accepts 5 sugar donors but not UDP-Api; mutants gained apiosylation activity
Genes.Perturbation_Type: Site-directed mutagenesis (insertion of Ser, His substitution in FFGDQ motif to mimic RLGSDH); crystal structure solved for WT and mutant
Genes.Quantitative_Effect: All three mutants catalyzed kaempferol into 3-O-apioside (confirmed by LC/MS, characteristic [Y0-H]⁻ ion at m/z 284, Fig. 6b)
Genes.Species: Scutellaria baicalensis (used as model for protein engineering)

Genes.Effect_Direction: Part of module 1; absence of module 1 → no flavonoid apioside production
Genes.Evidence_Text: For module 1, AtPAL, AtC4H, At4CL, AtCHS and GuCHR were used to synthesize isoliquiritigenin (2'). When module 1 or 2 was absent, no 2a could be generated (Fig. 8c).
Genes.Evidence_Type: Genetic perturbation + metabolite quantification (module presence/absence in tobacco)
Genes.Final_Nutrient_Product: Flavonoid apiosides (upstream pathway gene essential for de novo biosynthesis)
Genes.Pathway: Phenylpropanoid pathway; first committed step converting phenylalanine to cinnamic acid, upstream of flavonoid biosynthesis
Genes.Perturbation_Type: Transient expression in N. benthamiana (module 1); module removal experiment
Genes.Quantitative_Effect: Essential for de novo biosynthesis; module 1 absence → no 2a produced
Genes.Species: Arabidopsis thaliana (used in N. benthamiana de novo biosynthesis)

Genes.Effect_Direction: Part of module 1; absence of module 1 → no flavonoid apioside production
Genes.Evidence_Text: For module 1, AtPAL, AtC4H, At4CL, AtCHS and GuCHR were used to synthesize isoliquiritigenin (2'). When module 1 or 2 was absent, no 2a could be generated (Fig. 8c).
Genes.Evidence_Type: Genetic perturbation + metabolite quantification (module presence/absence in tobacco)
Genes.Final_Nutrient_Product: Flavonoid apiosides (upstream pathway gene essential for de novo biosynthesis)
Genes.Pathway: Phenylpropanoid pathway; hydroxylates cinnamic acid to p-coumaric acid
Genes.Perturbation_Type: Transient expression in N. benthamiana (module 1); module removal experiment
Genes.Quantitative_Effect: Essential for de novo biosynthesis as part of module 1
Genes.Species: Arabidopsis thaliana (used in N. benthamiana de novo biosynthesis)

Genes.Effect_Direction: Part of module 1; absence of module 1 → no flavonoid apioside production
Genes.Evidence_Text: For module 1, AtPAL, AtC4H, At4CL, AtCHS and GuCHR were used to synthesize isoliquiritigenin (2'). When module 1 or 2 was absent, no 2a could be generated (Fig. 8c).
Genes.Evidence_Type: Genetic perturbation + metabolite quantification (module presence/absence in tobacco)
Genes.Final_Nutrient_Product: Flavonoid apiosides (upstream pathway gene essential for de novo biosynthesis)
Genes.Pathway: Phenylpropanoid pathway; converts p-coumaric acid to coumaroyl-CoA
Genes.Perturbation_Type: Transient expression in N. benthamiana (module 1); module removal experiment
Genes.Quantitative_Effect: Essential for de novo biosynthesis as part of module 1
Genes.Species: Arabidopsis thaliana (used in N. benthamiana de novo biosynthesis)

Genes.Effect_Direction: Part of module 1; absence of module 1 → no flavonoid apioside production
Genes.Evidence_Text: For module 1, AtPAL, AtC4H, At4CL, AtCHS and GuCHR were used to synthesize isoliquiritigenin (2'). When module 1 or 2 was absent, no 2a could be generated (Fig. 8c).
Genes.Evidence_Type: Genetic perturbation + metabolite quantification (module presence/absence in tobacco)
Genes.Final_Nutrient_Product: Flavonoid apiosides (upstream pathway gene essential for de novo biosynthesis)
Genes.Gene_Role_Category: Key enzyme gene (type III polyketide synthase)
Genes.Pathway: Flavonoid biosynthesis; condenses coumaroyl-CoA and malonyl-CoA to form naringenin chalcone
Genes.Perturbation_Type: Transient expression in N. benthamiana (module 1); module removal experiment
Genes.Quantitative_Effect: Essential for de novo biosynthesis as part of module 1
Genes.Species: Arabidopsis thaliana (used in N. benthamiana de novo biosynthesis)

Title: Insights into the missing apiosylation step in flavonoid apiosides biosynthesis of Leguminosae plants
